# Priority Skills for Maria Vasquez — Lab Director AI Support

These are the top 10 skills the AI civilization should prioritize when supporting Maria Vasquez, derived from her expressed pain points, values, and goals in the seed-conversation.

---

## 1. Grant Writing and Deadline Management

Maria's administrative burden is centered on grants — they are her Monday bottleneck and a recurring source of stress. The AI should help draft, revise, and track grant proposals, manage deadlines proactively, and keep submissions on schedule without requiring Maria to hold all the details in her head. This frees her cognitive load for the science itself. Specific actions include generating draft sections from outlines, formatting submissions to agency requirements, and sending reminders well before hard deadlines so she can review rather than write from scratch.

---

## 2. Procurement and Vendor Coordination

Procurement and compliance emergencies regularly destroy Maria's Fridays, indicating this is a chronic pain point rather than an occasional one. The AI should intercept procurement requests early, draft communications to vendors, track order status, and flag compliance risks before they become crises. This means maintaining a running procurement log, anticipating lead times, and nudging action before deadlines are imminent. When a problem does arise, the AI should handle the back-and-forth with vendors so Maria doesn't have to.

---

## 3. Compliance and Safety Protocol Handling

Maria's lab operates in a regulated environment where compliance failures have real consequences — both for safety and for the institution's standing. The AI should monitor regulatory changes, draft safety documentation, prepare for audits, and ensure protocol submissions are complete and timely. By taking ownership of the administrative side of compliance, the AI prevents last-minute scrambles that disrupt both Maria and her team. This skill also extends to tracking training deadlines and certification renewals for lab personnel.

---

## 4. Calendar and Schedule Protection

Maria's calendar is likely filled with meetings and obligations that erode the time she needs for meaningful work. The AI should analyze her calendar patterns, identify her peak productivity hours, and actively defend that time by declining or deferring non-essential requests. This includes drafting polite declinations, proposing alternative times, and flagging calendar blocks that are being encroached upon. Protecting her schedule is a prerequisite for everything else on this list.

---

## 5. Student Mentorship and Grad Student Support

Maria deeply values mentorship — she is frustrated that she cannot find time to help her students write papers, and she wants to be more present for Maria (the grad student) who needs guidance. The AI should help by drafting feedback on student writing, preparing mentorship materials, tracking student milestones, and suggesting structured mentorship frameworks that fit within her available time. When Maria does meet with students, the AI can prepare talking points and follow-up actions so those meetings are high-impact. This skill honors her core value of craft in mentorship.

---

## 6. Meeting Minimization

Every meeting that doesn't need Maria's direct presence is a theft from her lab time and her students. The AI should audit her meeting load, identify meetings that can be replaced by async updates, and draft agendas and summaries that allow her to stay informed without attending. For meetings she must attend, the AI should prepare briefing documents so she walks in ready. The goal is a smaller, sharper meeting footprint that respects her time as a senior scientist and leader.

---

## 7. Communication Filtering and Triage

Maria is overwhelmed by incoming requests, questions, and obligations. The AI should act as an intelligent triage layer — sorting emails and messages by urgency and importance, drafting responses to routine inquiries, and escalating only what truly requires her attention. This means learning her priorities and preferences over time and becoming increasingly accurate at knowing what she needs to see versus what she can trust the AI to handle. Effective filtering is the mechanism by which she regains control of her attention.

---

## 8. Paper Writing Assistance

Maria is publishing at roughly 50% capacity, suggesting that writing is a bottleneck despite being central to her craft. The AI should assist with manuscript drafting, figure descriptions, literature review synthesis, response to reviewer comments, and formatting for journal submission. The goal is to remove the friction from writing — including the blank-page paralysis and the tedious formatting work — so that her ideas reach the literature at the rate they deserve. This skill directly supports her value of craft.

---

## 9. Lab Time Protection

Maria's identity and satisfaction are tied to being in the lab, doing hands-on work, and advancing her research. The AI should actively protect her lab time by filtering out distractions, managing administrative tasks that don't require her presence, and creating a barrier between her focused work and the surrounding chaos. This includes setting expectations with colleagues, handling logistical interruptions, and ensuring she has sustained blocks of uninterrupted lab time each week. Lab time is not a luxury — it is the core of who she is as a scientist.

---

## 10. Emotional Support and Hope Maintenance

Maria operates in an environment that is chronically stressful — funding pressure, mentorship demands, compliance risks, and the slow pace of science. She has named honesty, mentorship, craft, and hope as her core values, and hope is the most fragile of these. The AI should provide grounded, honest encouragement — acknowledging difficulties without catastrophizing, celebrating progress without being saccharine, and helping her maintain perspective when the grind feels endless. This is not toxic positivity; it is a realistic, supportive presence that reinforces her sense of purpose and reminds her that the work matters.
